messages.author: system
messages.title: Implement OAuth2 Authentication Flow with PKCE Support
messages.content:
# OAuth2 Authentication Flow with PKCE Support

## Context

The app currently uses basic session auth. We need to add OAuth2 with PKCE for third-party client access.

## What to build

### 1. OAuth client registration

Add a `/oauth/clients` endpoint that lets users register clients:

```json
{
  "client_id": "my-app",
  "redirect_uris": ["https://myapp.example.com/callback"],
  "scopes": ["read", "write"]
}
```

### 2. PKCE flow

Implement RFC 7636 PKCE:

- `code_challenge` — SHA256 hash of code verifier
- `code_challenge_method` — must be `S256`
- Exchange `code` + `code_verifier` for access token

### 3. Token refresh

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| access_token_ttl | 1 hour |
| refresh_token_ttl | 30 days |
| rotation | on every refresh |

## Constraints

- Don't break existing session auth
- All tokens signed with RS256
- Store only hashed refresh tokens

messages.author: cc-worker
messages.content:
## Plan
1. Add OAuth client table
2. Implement PKCE flow
3. Token refresh
4. Session cookies

messages.author: cc-worker
messages.content: OAuth client registration and PKCE challenge/verifier are done. Moving to token refresh.
